domain:
A Person has a name and an identifier. Every Person is the child of another Person and can have an indeterminate number of children (however, there will be one Person who is not the child of any other Person). A Person has an indeterminate number of ancestors (the ancestors of a Person are their father and the ancestors of their father). We will be interested in knowing the degree of each ancestor of a Person (1 for the father, 2 for the father's father, etc.).

A Resource has a name. There are two types of Resources, such as Files and Folders. Every resource belongs to a Folder and cannot exist outside of a Folder (however, there will be one Folder that does not follow that rule, the root Folder does not belong to any other Folder). Each Folder contains an indeterminate number of Resources.

A User is a Person who, in addition to a name and an identifier, also has a password to access an indeterminate number of Resources, and each Resource can be accessed by an indeterminate number of Users. When a User can access a resource, we will be interested in knowing if they can view it and if they can edit it.

questions.question:
Is ancestor degree a relevant domain concept that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Ancestor degree is a relevant concept in this domain and should be represented in the model.
2. Ancestor degree does not correspond to any relevant domain concept and should be removed from the model.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep ancestor degree, it is a relevant domain concept.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove ancestor degree, it is not relevant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep ancestor degree, it is a relevant domain concept.

questions.question:
Should ancestor degree be modeled as a separate class associated with Person, or as a simple representation within Person?

For example:
1. Ancestor degree is a separate class with attributes: degree. This allows users to query and filter by individual ancestor degree properties.
2. Ancestor degree is modeled as a simple represntation in Person. This is suitable when ancestor degree details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The AncestorDegree is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The ancestor degree is a simple representation in Person.
questions.selected: Option 1: The AncestorDegree is a separate class with its own attributes.

questions.question:
Is degree a relevant attribute of ancestor degree that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Degree captures important domain information about ancestor degree.
2. Degree does not represent important domain information for ancestor degree.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep degree, it is relevant information for ancestor degree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove degree, it is not a relevant information.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep degree, it is relevant information for ancestor degree.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Person in the relationship with AncestorDegree?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an ancestor degree can exist without any associated person.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an ancestor degree must have at least one associated person.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AncestorDegree can exist without Person.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,AncestorDegree must have at least one Person.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AncestorDegree can exist without Person.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Person in the relationship with AncestorDegree?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an ancestor degree is associated with exactly one person.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an ancestor degree can be associated with multiple people.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each ancestor degree links to one person.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each ancestor degree can link to multiple people.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each ancestor degree can link to multiple people.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of AncestorDegree in the relationship with Person?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a person can exist without any associated ancestor degree.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a person must have at least one associated ancestor degree.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Person can exist without AncestorDegree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Person must have at least one AncestorDegree.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Person can exist without AncestorDegree.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of AncestorDegree in the relationship with Person?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a person is associated with exactly one ancestor degree.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a person can be associated with multiple ancestor degrees.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each person links to one ancestor degree.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each person can link to multiple ancestor degrees.
questions.selected: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each person links to one ancestor degree.

questions.question:
Is view a relevant attribute of access permission that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. View captures important domain information about access permission.
2. View does not represent important domain information for access permission.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep view, it is relevant information for access permission.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove view, it is not a relevant information.
questions.selected: Option 2: Remove view, it is not a relevant information.

questions.question:
Is edit a relevant attribute of access permission that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Edit captures important domain information about access permission.
2. Edit does not represent important domain information for access permission.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep edit, it is relevant information for access permission.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove edit, it is not a relevant information.
questions.selected: Option 1: Keep edit, it is relevant information for access permission.

questions.question:
Should edits be modeled as a subclass of access permission, or is it better to keep a simple boolean like access permission in edit to distinguish them?
Consider whether edits have or will have distinct attributes or behavior.

For example:
1. Edit is a subclass of access permission. This allows edits to have their own specific attributes and methods.
2. Edit is an attribute in class access permission. All instances share the same structure, with the attribute indicating the type.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Use inheritance, Edit as a subclass ofaccess permission.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Use edit as a boolean attribute of  access permission.
questions.selected: Option 2: Use edit as a boolean attribute of  access permission.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of User in the relationship with AccessPermission?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an access permission can exist without any associated user.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an access permission must have at least one associated user.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AccessPermission can exist without User.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,AccessPermission must have at least one User.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AccessPermission can exist without User.

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an access permission is associated with exactly one user.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an access permission can be associated with multiple users.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each access permission links to one user.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each access permission can link to multiple users.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each access permission can link to multiple users.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of AccessPermission in the relationship with User?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a user can exist without any associated access permission.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a user must have at least one associated access permission.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, User can exist without AccessPermission.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,User must have at least one AccessPermission.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with AccessPermission?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an access permission can exist without any associated resource.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an access permission must have at least one associated resource.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AccessPermission can exist without Resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,AccessPermission must have at least one Resource.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, AccessPermission can exist without Resource.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of AccessPermission in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a resource is associated with exactly one access permission.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a resource can be associated with multiple access permissions.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each resource links to one access permission.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple access permissions.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple access permissions.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 2: Many as maximum cardinality, each resource can link to multiple access permissions.

Explanation: The domain says each Resource can be accessed by an indeterminate number of Users, and access permissions (view/edit) are recorded for when a User can access a Resource, so a Resource must be able to link to many AccessPermission entries.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Folder and Resource be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Resource would depend on Folder for its existence.
2. Use association, Resource could exist independently of Folder, and can also be linked to multiple folders.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Resource depends on Folder.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Resource exists independently of Folder.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Resource depends on Folder.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Folder and Folder be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Folder would depend on Folder for its existence.
2. Use association, Folder could exist independently of Folder, and can also be linked to multiple folders.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Folder depends on Folder.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Folder exists independently of Folder.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Folder depends on Folder.

questions.question:
Should AccessPermission be modeled as an association class linking User and Resource, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: AccessPermission exists only as part of the relationship between User and Resource. A access permission cannot exist without the link between User and Resource.
2. Regular class: AccessPermission is a class that has links to User and Resource. In this case multiple access permissions can exist for every User and Resource.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, AccessPermission as association class linking User and Resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, AccessPermission as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 2: Regular class, AccessPermission as separate class with two associations.
